title: Berkheya schinzii
intro: Berkheya schinzii, also known as the Cape daisy or bergamot daisy, is a perennial shrubby plant species within the daisy family (Asteraceae). Traditionally, the plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, making it useful for treating various health conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and wounds. The bioactive constituents of Berkheya schinzii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to prepare infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which are commonly used to treat colds, coughs, and digestive issues.
taxonomy: Berkheya schinzii is classified within the family Asteraceae. This plant belongs to the genus Berkheya, a group of flowering plants native to South Africa. The genus Berkheya is further grouped into the tribe Cichorieae. Berkheya schinzii is a member of the subtribe Cichoriinae within the tribe Cichorieae. It is closely related to other species within the same family, genus, and subtribe.
